perturbation.original_text: (o) "Company Field" means the design, development, manufacture, marketing, promotion, advertising, sourcing, distribution and sale of solid hardwood and engineered wood flooring products by or for any Company Entity.
perturbation.changed_text: (o) "Company Field" means the design, development, manufacture, marketing, promotion, advertising, sourcing, distribution and sale of solid hardwood flooring products, excluding engineered wood flooring, by or for any Company Entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by redefining the scope of the 'Company Field'. Originally, it included both solid hardwood and engineered wood flooring. The modification excludes 'engineered wood flooring', creating a conflict regarding which activities are covered by the licenses and assignments granted in the agreement. This specifically impacts the sections dealing with licensed IP, as certain provisions may now be interpreted differently based on whether they pertain to engineered wood flooring, contradicting the initial broader definition. The contradiction arises because several sections refer to 'Company Field' without specifying which definition is applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a perpetual, non- exclusive, royalty-free license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Copyrights for use in the Company Field throughout the world.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Company Field' by excluding 'engineered wood flooring'. The contradicted text grants a license for use in the 'Company Field', which originally included engineered wood flooring. Since the changed definition removes engineered wood flooring, it creates a direct contradiction regarding the scope of the license. Specifically, the license originally applied to 'solid hardwood and engineered wood flooring products' but now, after the change, the license only applies to 'solid hardwood flooring products'. This creates a clear conflict in determining whether the license granted in the 'Contradicted Text' applies to engineered wood flooring.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a limited, non- exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Arizona Licensed Trademark Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company an exclusive, royalty-free, non-sublicensable (except as set forth in Section 7.1), non-assignable license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Trademarks for the Arizona Trademark License Term for use in the Company Field throughout the world only in the form and manner that such Arizona Licensed Trademarks are used in the Business as of the Closing, provided that the Company shall use commercially reasonable efforts to present the Arizona Licensed Trademarks in the form set forth on Schedule 6.1.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the nature of the license granted to the Company for Arizona Licensed Trademarks. Originally, the license was 'non-exclusive,' allowing Arizona to also use the trademarks. The modification makes the license 'exclusive', restricting Arizona's own use of the trademarks in the Company Field. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 7.2, 'Reservation of Rights', states that 'each Party reserves its and its Affiliates' rights in and to all Intellectual Property (including with respect to the use, registration and licensing thereof).' Making the license exclusive directly conflicts with Arizona's reserved rights to use its own trademarks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly provided in the Stock Purchase Agreement or herein, each Party reserves its and its Affiliates' rights in and to all Intellectual Property (including with respect to the use, registration and licensing thereof).
perturbation.justification: The original text grants a 'non-exclusive' license, which aligns with Section 7.2's reservation of rights for Arizona to use its own intellectual property. The changed text, however, grants an 'exclusive' license, directly contradicting Section 7.2 because it restricts Arizona's ability to use its own trademarks, which it explicitly reserves the right to do. This creates a direct conflict between the grant in Section 6.1 and the reservation of rights in Section 7.2, making the agreement internally inconsistent and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated pursuant to the provisions hereof, the term of this Agreement and the licenses and other grants of rights (and related obligations) under this Agreement shall (i) with respect to the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, be for the Arizona Trademark License Term, (ii) with respect to the Diamond Licensed Trademarks, be for the Diamond Trademark License Term, (iii) with respect to the Phase- Out Marks, be for the term set forth in Section 6.6, and (iv) with respect to Copyrights, Know-How and Patents, be in perpetuity.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 Term and Termination. (a) Unless earlier terminated pursuant to the provisions hereof, the term of this Agreement and the licenses and other grants of rights (and related obligations) under this Agreement shall (i) with respect to the Arizona Licensed Trademarks, be for the Arizona Trademark License Term, (ii) with respect to the Diamond Licensed Trademarks, be for the Diamond Trademark License Term, (iii) with respect to the Phase- Out Marks, be for the term set forth in Section 6.6, and (iv) with respect to Copyrights, Know-How and Patents, be for five years.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the duration of the licenses for Copyrights, Know-How, and Patents. Originally, these licenses were granted 'in perpetuity,' meaning they would last indefinitely. The modification changes the term to 'five years', creating a direct conflict. This internal contradiction affects how long the Company can legally use the licensed Copyrights, Know-How, and Patents and it contradicts the sections that discuss perpetual licenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Arizona hereby grants to the Company a perpetual, non- exclusive, royalty-free license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Copyrights for use in the Company Field throughout the world.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the licenses for Copyrights, Know-How, and Patents are granted 'in perpetuity,' meaning indefinitely. The changed text modifies this to a term of 'five years.' This directly contradicts the section that states, 'Arizona hereby grants to the Company a perpetual... license in, to and under the Arizona Licensed Copyrights.' The change limits the license duration when the original agreement granted it perpetually, therefore, creating a direct contradiction.
